title: Identifying AI-Ready Tasks
description: Learn to evaluate everyday work tasks and determine which are good candidates for AI delegation. Covers the task suitability framework: repetitiveness, data availability, risk tolerance, and required judgment level.

title: The Delegation Decision Matrix
description: Interactive exercise where you classify 20 real-world tasks on two axes: AI capability and human oversight needed. Build intuition for when to automate, augment, or keep manual.

title: Breaking Down Complex Projects for AI
description: Master the art of decomposing multi-step projects into AI-suitable subtasks. Learn dependency mapping, handoff point identification, and how to sequence human and AI work for maximum efficiency.

title: Iterative Refinement Techniques
description: Practice the feedback loop cycle: evaluate AI output, diagnose gaps, give targeted feedback, and assess improvement. Includes 5 hands-on scenarios with progressively complex refinement challenges.

title: Choosing the Right AI Tool
description: Navigate the AI tool landscape with confidence. Compare capabilities of general-purpose LLMs, specialized AI tools, and custom models. Learn evaluation criteria for selecting the right tool for each task type.

title: Questioning AI Reasoning
description: Learn to probe AI decision-making by requesting explanations, challenging assumptions, and testing edge cases. Practice with scenarios in hiring, financial analysis, and strategic planning.

title: AI Delegation in High-Stakes Environments
description: Advanced strategies for delegating to AI in regulated industries, high-risk decisions, and time-critical situations. Covers governance frameworks, escalation protocols, and maintaining human accountability.

title: Building AI Workflow Orchestration
description: Design end-to-end workflows that chain multiple AI tools with human checkpoints. Simulate a multi-step research-to-report pipeline with quality gates and decision points.

title: Defining Scope and Guardrails for AI
description: Master techniques for setting clear boundaries on AI tasks: defining explicit success criteria, specifying out-of-scope areas, and creating guardrails that prevent AI drift.

title: Multi-Agent Delegation Strategies
description: Explore advanced patterns for orchestrating multiple AI agents on complex tasks. Learn about agent specialization, result aggregation, and conflict resolution between competing AI outputs.

title: Writing Your First Effective Prompt
description: Introduction to prompt engineering fundamentals. Learn the anatomy of a good prompt: task description, context, constraints, and output format. Practice with common workplace scenarios.

title: Context is King: Background Information in Prompts
description: Understand why context dramatically affects AI output quality. Learn what context to include, how to structure it, and common pitfalls of too little or too much context.

title: Specifying Output Format and Structure
description: Practice directing AI to produce outputs in specific formats: tables, JSON, bullet points, essays, emails, and more. Includes exercises on format specification and structure control.

title: Constraint Engineering for AI
description: Deep dive into specifying constraints effectively: word limits, tone requirements, technical accuracy levels, compliance boundaries, and audience-specific restrictions. Practice with regulated industry scenarios.

title: Few-Shot Prompting: Learning by Example
description: Master the technique of providing examples to guide AI behavior. Learn when few-shot prompting outperforms zero-shot, how to select representative examples, and how to use negative examples.

title: Role and Persona Setting
description: Learn how assigning roles and personas to AI changes output quality and perspective. Practice setting appropriate expertise levels, communication styles, and domain contexts.

title: Iterative Prompt Refinement Workshop
description: Hands-on simulation where you iteratively improve prompts across 8 scenarios. Start with a basic prompt, evaluate the output, diagnose issues, and refine until the output meets quality standards.

title: Advanced Prompt Architectures
description: Explore chain-of-thought prompting, self-consistency, tree-of-thought, and meta-prompting techniques. Learn when each approach adds value and practice with complex analytical tasks.

title: System Prompts and Prompt Templates
description: Design reusable prompt templates and system prompts for common organizational tasks. Build a personal prompt library with parameterized templates for reports, analysis, and communication.

title: Multi-Turn Conversation Design
description: Master the art of multi-turn AI interactions for complex tasks. Learn conversation planning, context management across turns, and techniques for maintaining coherence in long sessions.

title: Spotting AI Mistakes: A Beginner's Guide
description: Introduction to common AI errors: hallucinations, outdated information, logical inconsistencies, and confident falsehoods. Learn the red flags that signal AI output needs verification.

title: Fact-Checking AI Outputs
description: Step-by-step guide to verifying AI-generated claims, statistics, and citations. Learn efficient verification workflows and build the habit of checking before sharing.

title: Understanding AI Confidence vs. Accuracy
description: Learn why AI can sound completely confident while being completely wrong. Explore the difference between fluency and truthfulness, and develop calibrated trust in AI outputs.

title: Detecting Hallucinations in Practice
description: Interactive exercise with 15 AI outputs -- some contain hallucinations, some do not. Sharpen your ability to identify fabricated citations, invented facts, and plausible-sounding fiction.

title: Bias Detection in AI Outputs
description: Learn to identify systematic biases in AI responses: demographic bias, cultural bias, recency bias, and survivorship bias. Practice with real-world examples from hiring, lending, and content generation.

title: When AI Does Not Know What It Does Not Know
description: Develop the skill of recognizing when AI lacks context or expertise. Learn to spot hedge words, generic answers, and situations where AI fills knowledge gaps with plausible but unsupported content.

title: AI Limitations Deep Dive
description: Comprehensive exploration of fundamental AI limitations: training data cutoffs, context windows, reasoning vs. pattern matching, mathematical capabilities, and real-time knowledge gaps.

title: Critical Evaluation Frameworks for AI
description: Apply structured evaluation frameworks to AI outputs in high-stakes domains: legal analysis, medical information, financial advice, and engineering recommendations.

title: Red-Teaming AI Outputs
description: Advanced techniques for stress-testing AI outputs: adversarial questioning, edge case probing, cross-validation with multiple models, and systematic error analysis.

title: Building Organizational AI Verification Protocols
description: Design and implement team-level protocols for verifying AI outputs. Create checklists, review processes, and quality gates appropriate for different risk levels and use cases.

title: Responsible AI Use Essentials
description: Foundation course on ethical AI use in the workplace. Covers attribution, transparency, data privacy basics, and your responsibilities when using AI-generated content.

title: Data Privacy When Using AI
description: Learn what data should never be shared with AI tools, how to anonymize sensitive information, and how to evaluate AI tools' data handling policies. Includes PII identification exercises.

title: AI Attribution Best Practices
description: Practical guide to properly attributing AI contributions in different contexts: reports, presentations, code, creative work, and academic writing. Includes templates and examples.

title: Transparency in AI-Assisted Work
description: Learn when and how to disclose AI use to stakeholders, clients, and colleagues. Practice navigating awkward conversations about AI involvement in your work with confidence.

title: AI Ethics in Practice
description: Work through 10 ethical dilemmas involving AI use in the workplace. Develop a personal ethical framework for evaluating new AI use cases and their potential impacts.

title: Staying Current with AI Developments
description: Build a sustainable learning practice for keeping up with AI. Curate information sources, set up alerts, join communities, and create a personal AI learning roadmap.

title: Enterprise AI Governance Frameworks
description: Design and implement organizational AI governance: usage policies, approval workflows, risk classification, audit trails, and compliance monitoring for AI tool adoption.

title: AI Compliance in Regulated Industries
description: Navigate AI use in healthcare, finance, legal, and government contexts. Cover GDPR, HIPAA, SOX, and emerging AI-specific regulations with practical compliance checklists.

title: Building an AI-Ethical Culture
description: Lead organizational change toward responsible AI adoption. Develop training programs, establish review boards, create reporting mechanisms, and foster open dialogue about AI ethics.

title: AI Risk Assessment and Mitigation
description: Advanced techniques for assessing and mitigating AI-related risks in business operations. Covers risk matrices, impact analysis, mitigation strategies, and incident response planning.
